sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The grid is all g again! It seems like clicking multiple cells in a batch caused a reset. Let me think about what happened:
1. I clicked (50,30) - painted N successfully
2. Then I batched 3 clicks: (55,25), (55,35), (60,30)
3. Result: all cells became g
This suggests that when I batch multiple clicks, something goes wrong. Maybe:
- Each click changes the cursor state, so subsequent clicks don't work as expected
- Or there's some interaction between batched clicks
Let me try clicking cells one at a time, checking the state after each click. Also, I need to understand why the grid resets.
Actually, looking at the pattern:
- When I clicked (55,30) earlier (center cell), the grid reset
- Now when I batched 3 clicks, the grid also reset
Maybe the issue is that after painting one cell, the cursor state changes and subsequent clicks with the wrong cursor state cause a reset. Or maybe clicking a cell that's already g when the cursor is in a certain state causes a reset.
Let me try a different approach: click cells one at a time, checking the state after each click.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Grid reset to all g after batched clicks. Need to understand why - possibly cursor state changes between clicks or batched clicks cause issues.
Recent findings: Single click works but batched clicks reset the grid. Need to click one at a time.
Plan: Click cells one at a time, checking state after each click.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Cursor: {ascii_lines[19][10:45]}")

# Click one cell at a time
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30}])
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"After (50,30): cursor={ascii_lines[19][35:45]}")
grid = [ascii_lines[r][c] for r in [50,55,60] for c in [25,30,35]]
print(f"Grid: {grid}")

result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25}])
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"After (55,25): cursor={ascii_lines[19][35:45]}")
grid = [ascii_lines[r][c] for r in [50,55,60] for c in [25,30,35]]
print(f"Grid: {grid}")

result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35}])
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"After (55,35): cursor={ascii_lines[19][35:45]}")
grid = [ascii_lines[r][c] for r in [50,55,60] for c in [25,30,35]]
print(f"Grid: {grid}")

result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30}])
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"After (60,30): cursor={ascii_lines[19][35:45]}")
grid = [ascii_lines[r][c] for r in [50,55,60] for c in [25,30,35]]
print(f"Grid: {grid}")
print(f"Done: {last_action_result.get('done')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
